location.office_time_required: minimum of three days in the office per week, Monday Wednesday and Thursday

level_signals: Leads a team of highly technical operators
Comp range $210,600–$263,250 base, consistent with Senior Manager at a late-stage fintech

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5+ years of experience leading Systems, BizOps, TechOps, Internal Platform, or Operational Excellence teams in high-growth, fast-paced environments.
Proven track record of building and scaling internal systems, workflows, and operational tooling that drive measurable business impact.
Experience leveraging AI/LLM technologies to automate workflows, improve operational efficiency, or build internal tools and agentic systems.
Strong understanding of modern operational tooling ecosystems, including platforms such as Salesforce, Retool, workflow automation tools, and data infrastructure.
Technical fluency with systems architecture and automation development, including working knowledge of JavaScript, Python, SQL, APIs, and AI tooling ecosystems.
Strong analytical and operational mindset with the ability to use data to inform strategy, prioritize investments, and measure business outcomes.
Demonstrated ability to operate effectively across highly cross-functional environments and align technical solutions with business strategy.
Proven ability to navigate ambiguity, drive alignment across stakeholders, and lead complex initiatives from concept to execution.
Strong ownership mentality with a bias toward action and a willingness to operate hands-on when needed.
Excitement for building the next generation of AI-native operational systems and workflows.

scope.surface: Systems and operational infrastructure powering Brex's Operations organization — including Risk, Compliance, Payment Operations, Servicing, and Banking Operations
scope.business_model: B2B fintech — corporate cards, banking, spend management, bill pay, and travel software
primary_metrics_inferred: Measurable business impact of internal systems and tooling
core_responsibilities: Lead a team of highly technical operators responsible for systems, automation, and operational infrastructure
Define long-term vision for how Brex scales operationally
Drive execution on high-impact systems, workflows, and AI-powered tooling
Transform traditional operations into AI-native organizations through intelligent automation, agentic workflows, decision infrastructure, and scalable internal platforms
Partner with Product, Engineering, Data, Finance, and Operations leaders to solve complex business problems

compensation.equity_form: equity and other forms of compensation may be provided as part of a total compensation package

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed and competitive ($210,600–$263,250 base)
Clear ownership area — Operations systems across Risk, Compliance, Payment Operations, Servicing, Banking Operations
Explicit hybrid policy with structured in-office days and up to 4 weeks fully remote as a perk
High company visibility and cross-functional scope
Role sits at intersection of AI transformation and operational scale — high strategic importance
red_flags: Hybrid requirement mandates three specific days (Mon/Wed/Thu) in San Francisco office — highly restrictive for non-local candidates
No mention of visa sponsorship or work authorization — unclear for international applicants
Buzzword-heavy JD (AI-native, agentic, connective tissue repeated) with limited specificity on actual deliverables or success metrics
Responsibilities section partially repeats the 'Systems at Brex' intro verbatim — signals possible JD template/copy-paste issue

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from title 'Senior Manager', comp range, and team leadership signals)
seniority.manages_people (inferred from 'lead a team of highly technical operators')
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not in JD)

ambiguities: The 'Systems at Brex' section and 'Responsibilities' section contain nearly identical text — unclear if this is intentional or a copy-paste error in the JD.
No nice-to-haves or preferred qualifications are stated; all requirements treated as hard per JD language.
Work authorization and visa sponsorship are not mentioned anywhere in the JD.
